title: Configure the Use of Translation Tables
breadcrumbs: Configure the Use of Translation Tables
text: Configure the Use of Translation Tables The default USYS library usually includes all the translation tables needed to interface with display and keyboard devices, so there is no need for special configuration. However, if you have defined your own translation tables for specific devices, you can specify the library that holds them. If you are not using a translation table from the USYS library, specify the library using the $VARIATION setting. Optionally, specify specific translation tables you want to use using the following assignment settings: display —set the display translation table $keyboard —specify the keyboard translation table $DEFAULT_TERM —specify the default terminal setting to be used if the defined device translation table cannot be found. Related Links Translation Tables To mediate between itself and the external environment, Uniface uses translation tables. Translation tables provide input and output mappings between platform-specific device codes and Uniface functions, and between external and internal character sets. Uniface provides translation tables for the platforms it supports, but you can customize these or use them to create your own tables. $VARIATION Specify the library name for global objects needed by the application when the application starts. Libraries are typed, so each global object library can have the same name which functions as a shared namespace which can be specified by this setting. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
